manifest.title: Refractured Commercial Strategy Archive v2
manifest.description: Static backup mirror of the archived v2 report snapshot. The primary client experience is the interactive Indievaders route #client-report/refractured.

sources.label: Curse of the Dead Gods Steam page
PC Gamer June 2026 Next Fest coverage
GamesRadar Slay the Spire 2 launch coverage
Steam review API attempt
sources.path: Tool attempt on 2026-06-20 failed in the local Windows TLS environment; store aggregates and public pages were used instead.

sources.label: Reddit and Discord social listening gap
sources.path: Logged-in social listening was not completed in this pass.

cards.description: Composite strategic read from local premise, public market evidence, and current prototype stage. Directional, not a revenue forecast.
Recommended first path, scored as a first proof loop rather than final production ambition.
Pre-Steam success metric for proving buyer intent through playable evidence.

charts.title: Market-fit scorecard
charts.subtitle: Directional 1-5 scores from reviewed public evidence and local premise fit.

query.sql: SELECT dimension, score, confidence, evidence_level FROM market_scorecard
query.description: Loads the directional market-fit scorecard from the reviewed report snapshot.
filters: Refractured v2 report evidence reviewed on 2026-06-20
metric_definitions: score: directional 1-5 strategic score; confidence: fractional confidence in the supporting evidence

charts.title: Review pressure themes
charts.subtitle: Themes to validate in playtests before public Steam positioning.

query.sql: SELECT theme, reward_weight, punish_risk, primary_segment, validation_signal FROM review_theme_map
query.description: Loads review pressure themes inferred from public comparable evidence and stakeholder review.
filters: Qualitative public evidence only; no completed Reddit or Discord sample
metric_definitions: reward_weight and punish_risk: directional 1-5 relative weights for early validation

charts.title: Pre-Steam proof funnel
charts.subtitle: Use playtest and campaign signals before treating wishlists as demand proof.

query.sql: SELECT stage, target_signal_strength, measure, source_or_tool FROM steam_funnel ORDER BY funnel_order
query.description: Loads pre-Steam proof funnel stages from the report KPI framework.
filters: Pre-Steam and Steam-page readiness metrics only
metric_definitions: target_signal_strength: directional 1-5 strength of evidence needed before scaling production or marketing

tables.title: Comparable evidence matrix
tables.subtitle: Use references as expectation maps, not as sales forecasts.

query.sql: SELECT game, lane, steam_signal, relevance_score, borrow, avoid FROM competitor_matrix ORDER BY relevance_score DESC
query.description: Loads comparable evidence rows from public Steam pages and public coverage.
filters: Comparable games relevant to 2.5D, beat 'em up, action roguelite, dark action, or premium Steam positioning

tables.title: Player segment map
tables.subtitle: The report should say who is buying the fantasy and how they judge it.

query.sql: SELECT priority, segment, rewards, punishes, validation_metric FROM player_segments ORDER BY priority
query.description: Loads player segment hypotheses from local premise and public comparable evidence.
filters: Pre-Steam target segments for Refractured commercial validation

tables.title: KPI framework
tables.subtitle: Pre-Steam metrics should prove the hook, the run, and the player intent.

query.sql: SELECT priority, metric, definition, decision_use, target_range, evidence_status FROM kpi_framework ORDER BY priority
query.description: Loads the KPI framework for pre-Steam Refractured validation.
filters: Pre-Steam proof metrics and future Steam UTM metrics
metric_definitions: Qualified Wishlist Intent from Playable Proof combines hook comprehension, second-run desire, and stated wishlist intent from the target player segment

tables.title: Strategic path decision gates
tables.subtitle: Path A is the first test; Path B or C should be earned by player evidence.

query.sql: SELECT gate_order, gate, pass_signal, path_trigger, failure_response FROM decision_gates ORDER BY gate_order
query.description: Loads strategic path gates that convert playtest evidence into scope decisions.
filters: Path A/B/C strategy for Refractured report v2

tables.title: Evidence confidence ledger
tables.subtitle: Major claims are labeled by confidence and source type.

query.sql: SELECT confidence_rank, claim, evidence_type, confidence, source_label, report_use FROM evidence_ledger ORDER BY confidence_rank DESC
query.description: Loads the evidence confidence ledger for auditability.
filters: Claims used in Refractured report v2

tables.title: 30 / 90 / 365 day plan
tables.subtitle: The plan turns positioning into measurable proof instead of long speculative production.

query.sql: SELECT period_order, period, objective, deliverable, success_signal FROM roadmap ORDER BY period_order
query.description: Loads the phased commercial validation plan.

blocks.body:
# Refractured Commercial Strategy Archive v2

Status: Static backup mirror

Primary experience: `#client-report/refractured`

This archived artifact snapshot is not the current client report surface.

## Commercial Verdict

- **Refractured has a real commercial lane if it sells a brutal 2.5D action roguelite, not a generic brawler.** Local project docs confirm the recurring nightmare or ritual loop as a core premise, and public Steam evidence shows that beat 'em up plus roguelite is viable when combat feel and replay value are credible.
- **Path A is the best first market test, not the final ambition ceiling.** The next proof should be a solo-first nightmare micro-run that tests whether players want one more run before the team commits to larger content, co-op, or deep mastery systems.
- **The buyer will judge contact, fairness, and run texture before scope.** The highest-risk complaints are floaty combat, unclear pressure, generic roguelite upgrades, content-thin value, and misleading solo/co-op framing.
- **The product should be measured before it is scaled.** The north star is Qualified Wishlist Intent from Playable Proof: players understand the hook, want a second run, and would wishlist because of the playable fantasy, not because the pitch sounds promising.

## Archived Readiness Snapshot

**The opportunity is the overlap between loyal beat 'em up players and action roguelite players who want mastery.** This retained chart data is directional archive material. The current client experience lives in the interactive route, where these signals can be reviewed in context with updated modules.

## Comparable Evidence

**Absolum is the closest modern signal because it directly combines beat 'em up and roguelite language.** Rotwood shows the risk of mixed solo/co-op framing and content expectations. Dead Cells and Hades II set the broader action roguelite bar for replay clarity and polish, but Refractured should not invite a direct comparison unless the demo earns it.

## Player Segments

**A brutal 2.5D action roguelite is judged by several overlapping buyers.** The beat 'em up loyalist is unforgiving about hit feel and collision. The roguelite optimizer audits fairness and build behavior. The dark-action buyer needs style and ritual identity. Creators need a clip-worthy moment, while publishers need proof that the hook can become a repeatable demand loop.

## Archived Roguelite Proof Notes

The archived v2 snapshot framed replay proof around fast retry, run-local ritual choices, fair pressure, meta progression only after the run is fun, and content density after repeat desire is proven. Use the interactive route for the current module-level treatment.

## Archived Review Theme Notes

**The most useful review themes are not generic sentiment; they are failure modes that can be measured in playtests.** This archive keeps the v2 theme data for traceability; the current client-facing review should happen in the interactive route.

## Strategic Path Decision Tree

**The recommendation is conditional.** Start with Path A because it answers the hardest commercial question fastest: does the fight make people want another run? Move toward Path B only if players ask for more routes, enemies, content, and co-op. Move toward Path C only if combat mastery becomes the reason people return and funding supports the burden.

## KPI Framework

**Pre-Steam KPIs should connect gameplay proof to Steam demand.** Steamworks UTM reporting can later measure campaign visits, wishlist conversions, purchases, and activations, but before the Steam page exists the team needs controlled playtest metrics that show whether the promise is understood and repeatable.

## Steam Demo / Wishlist Funnel

**Wishlist intent should come after hook, combat feel, and second-run proof.** The funnel below shows how the report turns a pre-Steam concept into source-attributed demand signals.

## 30 / 90 / 365 Day Plan

**The short plan is not to shrink ambition; it is to earn bigger ambition with evidence.** A capture-ready arena, then a 3-room micro-run, then a measured Steam/demo funnel gives the team more leverage than a long private build with no market signal.

## Evidence Confidence Ledger

- **No sales forecast is claimed.** Pricing and revenue remain strategic hypotheses until content volume, traffic, wishlist behavior, and conversion data exist.
- **Steam review API sampling was attempted but unavailable in this local environment.** Store-page aggregate sentiment, public reviews, Steamworks docs, and public market coverage were used instead.
- **Logged-in Reddit, Discord, and creator-comment mining are not completed evidence.** They should be the next research upgrade before this becomes a repeatable premium report template.
- **The biggest open question is the lead promise.** Refractured can combine brutality, roguelite replay, and nightmare ritual identity, but the Steam page and trailer need one of those to lead.

market_scorecard.why_it_matters: Local docs already frame Refractured as a combat-first action roguelite with nightmare or ritual loop.
Beat 'em up plus roguelite is a recognizable lane, but needs a sharper hook than genre labels.
Brutal physicality plus nightmare ritual can avoid generic Hades-like positioning.
The current commercial risk is whether combat feel creates second-run desire before scope expands.
Premium Steam first is coherent, but price depends on content density and review confidence.
Reddit, Discord, creator comments, and broader review mining remain incomplete.

competitor_matrix.steam_signal: Released Oct 2025; Steam page shows Very Positive English reviews, demo, Beat 'em up and Roguelite tags.
competitor_matrix.borrow: Use the hybrid lane, demo logic, impact clarity, and run-based brawler framing.
competitor_matrix.avoid: Avoid copying fantasy tone or assuming content volume can be lighter without review risk.

competitor_matrix.steam_signal: Released Mar 2026; Steam page shows strong English lifetime sentiment but mixed recent sentiment.
competitor_matrix.borrow: Use combat mastery and gear/build learning as proof of replay value.
competitor_matrix.avoid: Avoid ambiguous solo/co-op promise and content pacing issues.

competitor_matrix.steam_signal: Long-running Steam benchmark with Overwhelmingly Positive English reviews and clear kill, die, learn, repeat promise.
competitor_matrix.borrow: Use clear loop language, fast retry, and permanent learning as marketing clarity.
competitor_matrix.avoid: Avoid inviting content-depth comparison before Refractured earns it.

competitor_matrix.steam_signal: Major Steam benchmark for replayability, polish, and high expectation bar.
competitor_matrix.borrow: Use the clarity of a repeatable fantasy and strong run structure.
competitor_matrix.avoid: Avoid Hades-like positioning unless narrative, content, and polish justify it.

competitor_matrix.steam_signal: Steam page positions solo and up-to-4 co-op action roguelike with hero variety.
competitor_matrix.borrow: Use hero/build variety and replayability as expectation references.
competitor_matrix.avoid: Avoid co-op-first tuning if Refractured is selling solo-first brutality.

competitor_matrix.steam_signal: Steam page positions skill, curses, relics, weapons, traps, and dark temple runs.
competitor_matrix.borrow: Use fair danger, curses, and dark mechanical identity as reference points.
competitor_matrix.avoid: Avoid repetition and run-length friction without strong combat payoffs.

player_segments.rewards: Immediate hit impact, readable depth collision, enemy-wave rhythm, fair contact, strong animations.
player_segments.punishes: Floaty hits, cheap damage, unclear z-axis collision, shallow enemy roles.
player_segments.validation_metric: Combat feel score and unclear-damage complaint rate.

player_segments.rewards: Fast retry, meaningful build changes, fair RNG, auditable rules, mastery across runs.
player_segments.punishes: Flat stat upgrades, grind, unpredictable unfairness, low run variety.
player_segments.validation_metric: Second-run intent and behavior-changing build recall.

player_segments.rewards: Brutal tone, ritual identity, audio/visual violence tied to mechanics, memorable enemy threat.
player_segments.punishes: Gore without consequence, generic fantasy, muddy readability.
player_segments.validation_metric: Hook recall after 30-45 seconds of gameplay footage.

player_segments.rewards: Clip-worthy reversal, visible perfect read, brutal punish, surprising second-run change.
player_segments.punishes: Slow setup, menu-heavy systems, unclear stakes, non-visual progression.
player_segments.validation_metric: Shareable moment recall and creator pitch response.

player_segments.rewards: Clear market lane, measurable proof loop, scope gates, Steam funnel plan, evidence discipline.
player_segments.punishes: Unbounded ambition, no telemetry, genre confusion, unsupported sales claims.
player_segments.validation_metric: Decision gate pass/fail and evidence confidence ledger completeness.

review_theme_map.validation_signal: Players describe hits as heavy, readable, and fair without prompting.
Low unclear-damage complaint rate during crowded encounters.
Players voluntarily start a second run or ask what changes next.
Players can name how a ritual choice changed their play.
Players accept a focused demo because it is replayable, not because they imagine future content.
Solo players feel the arena, camera, and pacing were tuned for them.
Players remember nightmare ritual identity more than generic genre labels.
A short clip communicates threat, read, punish, and changed next run.

strategic_paths.requires: One excellent arena, then a 3-room micro-run with 8-12 behavior-changing ritual choices.
strategic_paths.upside: Proves hook, combat feel, replay desire, and Steam page promise before large production.
strategic_paths.risk: Can look small if the demo is not dense, polished, and replayable.

strategic_paths.commercial_role: Scale when players ask for breadth
strategic_paths.condition: Choose if playtests ask for routes, enemy variety, character options, and co-op or content depth.
strategic_paths.requires: Content pipeline, enemy role library, route structure, progression tuning, possible co-op clarity.
strategic_paths.upside: Supports higher premium price and broader repeat play if content density is real.
strategic_paths.risk: Solo identity can blur and content expectations rise fast.

strategic_paths.path: Path C: Deep Combat Mastery Platform
strategic_paths.condition: Choose only if combat mastery becomes the return reason and funding supports depth.
strategic_paths.requires: Advanced enemies, teaching UX, mastery systems, balance cadence, long-tail updates.
strategic_paths.upside: Can become a durable premium action roguelite if the combat system earns loyalty.
strategic_paths.risk: Highest proof burden; weak core feel will be exposed immediately.

decision_gates.pass_signal: 70%+ of target players can describe the hook as brutal 2.5D action roguelite with ritual/retry identity.
decision_gates.path_trigger: Stay Path A and prepare capture-ready arena.
decision_gates.failure_response: Rewrite promise, trailer beat, and Steam tags before expanding systems.

decision_gates.pass_signal: Players rate contact and readability high, with low unclear-damage complaints.
decision_gates.failure_response: Fix combat feel, hit reaction, enemy tells, camera, and collision before adding progression.

decision_gates.pass_signal: 50%+ of qualified players voluntarily retry or say they want another run.
decision_gates.path_trigger: Add ritual choice layer and controlled run variation.
decision_gates.failure_response: Tighten room pacing, retry loop, and run-local decisions.

decision_gates.pass_signal: Players can explain how a ritual choice changed tactics, not just stats.
decision_gates.path_trigger: Keep Path A or graduate toward Path C if mastery drives repeat play.
decision_gates.failure_response: Replace flat modifiers with behavior-changing choices.

decision_gates.pass_signal: Positive players ask for routes, enemies, weapons, or co-op after the core loop works.
decision_gates.path_trigger: Consider Path B with a scoped content plan.
decision_gates.failure_response: Hold content expansion until retention signals are real.

decision_gates.pass_signal: Steam page or campaign traffic converts into qualified wishlist intent with clear source attribution.
decision_gates.path_trigger: Decide premium launch, Early Access, or publisher pitch timing.
decision_gates.failure_response: Improve capsule, trailer, demo CTA, and traffic source targeting.

kpi_framework.metric: Qualified Wishlist Intent from Playable Proof
kpi_framework.definition: Share of target players who understand the hook, want another run, and would wishlist because of the playable demo or clip.
kpi_framework.target_range: 30-40%+ in qualified closed tests before broad public push.

kpi_framework.definition: Percent of viewers or players who can describe the fantasy after 30-45 seconds without being coached.
kpi_framework.decision_use: Validates trailer, Steam capsule, tags, and one-line promise.

kpi_framework.definition: Percent of players who voluntarily retry, ask what changes next, or report wanting another run after failure or completion.
kpi_framework.decision_use: Validates roguelite loop before content expansion.

kpi_framework.definition: Average player rating of hit impact, input response, enemy readability, camera, and depth collision.
kpi_framework.decision_use: Blocks false positives from good concept but weak feel.
kpi_framework.target_range: 4.0/5 or better with low unclear-damage complaints.

kpi_framework.definition: Percent of players who can name how a run-local choice changed their tactics.
kpi_framework.decision_use: Tests whether roguelite choices alter behavior instead of stats only.

kpi_framework.definition: Wishlist additions within Steamworks UTM reporting windows by campaign source once the store page exists.
kpi_framework.decision_use: Connects creator/demo traffic to measurable demand.
kpi_framework.target_range: Set after first campaign baseline; avoid inventing before traffic exists.

steam_funnel.source_or_tool: Playtest survey or creator outreach form

roadmap.deliverable: Capture-ready arena clip showing threat, read, punish, and retry fantasy.
roadmap.success_signal: Target players understand the hook and describe the game with intended words.

roadmap.deliverable: 3-room micro-run with one post-room ritual choice and quick retry.
roadmap.success_signal: Players voluntarily want another run and remember how the ritual changed play.

roadmap.deliverable: Steam page draft, demo CTA, creator pitch, and UTM plan.
roadmap.success_signal: Qualified wishlist intent and source-attributed traffic start to emerge.

roadmap.deliverable: Decision on Path A launch scope, Path B content expansion, or Path C funding-backed mastery platform.
roadmap.success_signal: Decision gates show which ambition is earned by player evidence.

evidence_ledger.claim: Refractured is intended as a combat-first action roguelite with nightmare or ritual loop.
evidence_ledger.report_use: Sets core premise and prevents generic brawler framing.

evidence_ledger.claim: Steam visibility depends on customer response such as purchasing, playing, reviewing, and related factors.
evidence_ledger.report_use: Supports focus on playable proof and demand signals.

evidence_ledger.claim: Steam Early Access is not a crowdfunding substitute and must deliver a playable game at purchase.
evidence_ledger.report_use: Frames Early Access as conditional, not default financing.

evidence_ledger.claim: Steam Next Fest requires a public trailer, demo readiness, updated store page, and accurate tags/categories.
evidence_ledger.report_use: Shapes demo and Steam page readiness plan.

evidence_ledger.claim: Absolum confirms an active roguelite beat 'em up lane on Steam.
Rotwood shows the promise and risk of co-op combat roguelite framing.
June 2026 Next Fest discovery pressure is high due to thousands of demos and AI-disclosure noise.
evidence_ledger.source_label: GamesRadar and PC Gamer Next Fest coverage
evidence_ledger.report_use: Supports need for sharp first 5 seconds and clear demo hook.

evidence_ledger.claim: Beat 'em up players are especially sensitive to hit feel, collision, readability, and cheap damage.
evidence_ledger.source_label: Comparable store pages and public review themes
evidence_ledger.report_use: Defines player segment and validation metrics.

evidence_ledger.claim: Qualified Wishlist Intent is the right pre-Steam north star.
evidence_ledger.source_label: Steamworks UTM and current project stage
evidence_ledger.report_use: KPI framework; needs real telemetry later.

evidence_ledger.claim: Logged-in Reddit and Discord sentiment for this exact niche was not completed in this pass.
evidence_ledger.report_use: Evidence gap for the next research pass.

evidence_ledger.claim: Steam review API theme sampling was attempted but unavailable in this local environment.
evidence_ledger.report_use: Uses public store aggregates instead; API sampling remains an evidence gap.
